revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Amatsuhikone]][[fa:آماتسوهیکونه]][[fr:Amatsuhikone]][[it:Amatsuhikone]][[ja:アマツヒコネ]][[zh:天津日子根命]]


'''Amatsuhikone''' (天津日子根命,天津彥根命, which means little lad of Heaven) in Japanese mythology is the third son of [[Amaterasu]].{{Citation needed|date=May 2024|qq=}}
In many versions, he is born from Amaterasu's jewels in her hair.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book|last=Japanese (U.S.)|first=Association of Teachers of|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5cqAQAAIAAJ&q=Amatsuhikone|title=The Journal of the Association of Teachers of Japanese|date=1990|publisher=The Association|language=en|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Nobutaka|first=Inoue|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LgQRAQAAIAAJ&q=Amatsuhikone|title=Kami|date=March 1998|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics, Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-05-3|language=en|qq=}}</ref> In other versions he is born from the vine used to bind Amaterasu's hair.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Amatsuhikone|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en|qq=}}</ref>
{{Infobox deity
| type = Shinto
| name = Amatsuhikone
| siblings = [[Ame no Hohi]] (brother)
{{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (brother)
{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|qid=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone|1=Ikutsuhikone}} (brother)
[[:en:Hinohayahi|Hinohayahi]] (brother)
| mother = [[Amaterasu]]
| children = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}}
| image = File:Amatsuhikone shinbutsu-zue.jpg
| gender = {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}
| parents ={{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}| offspring = {{ill|Ame no Mahitotsu no kami|qid=Q10016750|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|qid=Q134879671|1=Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami}}
| relatives = {{ill|Q11436785|qid=Q11436785|1=Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}; {{ill|Q11486843|qid=Q11486843|1=Takekoro-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Ōshikōchi no Mitsune|qid=Q385896|1=Ōshikōchi no Mitsune}}
}}
He is believed to be the ancestor of several clans, including the Oshikochi and Yamashiro clans.<ref name=":1" /> 
{{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}} is his son. He laterdescended onto 432-meter {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|qid=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami|1=Mount Mikami}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|qid=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.<ref name=":02">[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine]</ref><ref name=":12">{{Cite web |last=KansaiOdyssey |date=2020-10-15 |title=Mikami Shrine: The Treasure of Yasu, Shiga |url=https://kansai-odyssey.com/mikami-shrine-yasu-shiga-prefecture/ |access-date=2024-06-07 |website=Kansai Odyssey |language=en-US|qq=}}</ref> On the mountain [[Mikami Shrine]] was built to worship him. It is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|qid=Q611642|lt=Yasu|1=Yasu, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref name=":02" /><ref name=":12" />
[[File:Children born from the Challenge of Amaterasu and Susanoo-en.svg|thumb|240x240px|Oath between Amaterasu and Susanowo (based on the Kojiki)]]
